name: Kaiperm
description_short: Kaiperm is a not-for-profit credit union exclusively serving Kaiser Permanente employees, retirees, and families nationwide since 1957, offering competitive rates and fee-free banking.
description_long:
Kaiperm Credit Union was founded in August 1957 by physicians, administrators, and employees of Kaiser Permanente in Walnut Creek, California, operating on the principle of "people helping people." Originally named Kaiperm Walnut Creek Federal Credit Union, the organization expanded its charter in 2022 to serve Kaiser Permanente employees and their families nationwide. As of 2026, Kaiperm serves over 7,800 members and operates as a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution regulated by the NCUA.

Kaiperm offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking and lending services tailored to its membership base. Members can access free checking accounts with zero maintenance fees, savings accounts, IRAs, certificates of deposit, and auto loans with competitive rates. The credit union provides access to 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide and mobile banking capabilities for real-time account management and loan applications. Kaiperm also facilitates member discounts through partnerships with retailers like Santa Cruz Beach Boardwalk and Get Away Today for travel planning.

Kaiperm distinguishes itself through its restricted membership model—eligibility is limited to Kaiser Permanente employees, retirees, volunteers, and their family members. This membership requirement allows the credit union to maintain laser focus on serving a specific employee demographic while leveraging their shared employer relationship. The not-for-profit structure eliminates many fees associated with traditional banks and enables lower loan rates. Founded by and for healthcare professionals, Kaiperm emphasizes long-term member relationships and financial wellness.

Kaiperm is a legitimate, NCUA-insured credit union with verifiable operational history and transparent membership criteria. However, prospective members must first qualify for Kaiser Permanente employment or family affiliation. The website provides limited specific rate information, requiring members to contact the credit union directly for loan pricing and savings account yields. As a smaller regional credit union, Kaiperm may offer fewer digital tools and branches compared to national competitors, though partnership networks partially offset this limitation.
services: Free checking accounts with zero maintenance fees
Auto loans with special EV and hybrid vehicle financing
Online account access and real-time balance checking
pros: Free checking accounts with zero maintenance fees
Access to 30,000 surcharge-free ATMs nationwide via partnership network
Lower loan rates and fewer fees due to not-for-profit status
Competitive auto loan rates with special financing for electric and hybrid vehicles
Member-exclusive discounts through partnerships (Santa Cruz Boardwalk, Get Away Today)
Mobile banking app for account management and loan applications
NCUA-insured deposits protecting up to $250,000 per account category
Long operational history since 1957 demonstrating institutional stability
cons: Membership restricted to Kaiser Permanente employees, retirees, volunteers, and their families—not open to general public
Website lacks specific APR ranges, loan terms, and savings account yields, requiring direct contact for rate information
Limited branch network implied by regional focus on Walnut Creek origins, potentially requiring online-only banking for non-local members
Smaller institution may offer fewer digital features and investment products compared to large national credit unions
Member base of 7,800 is relatively small, limiting scale and potentially affecting service availability
best_for: Kaiser Permanente employees seeking employer-affiliated banking with competitive rates and reduced fees
Kaiser Permanente retirees and family members wanting member-exclusive discounts and not-for-profit lending
Healthcare professionals at Kaiser Permanente interested in long-term financial relationships and wellness-focused banking
Kaiser employees planning vehicle purchases, especially electric or hybrid cars, through specialized auto loan programs
diagnosis: Kaiperm is best for Kaiser Permanente employees and their families seeking accessible, member-focused banking with competitive rates and minimal fees. The primary caveat is strict membership eligibility—this credit union is exclusively for the Kaiser Permanente employee community and cannot serve the general public, making it unavailable to most consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Kaiperm — Credit Union in Walnut Creek, CA | CreditDoc
meta_description: Kaiperm — NCUA-insured credit union in Walnut Creek, CA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
